dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson c †Ishan Kishan b Cummins 27 (13b 5x4 1x6 14m) SR: 207.69

dismissalComment.html: Big appeal from Cummins and Kishan for the caught behind and the umpire agrees. Samson isn't sure and reviews. I think I heard a noise. Let's see. On a length just outside off, nibbling away from Samson, who pushes at it and, yep, there is a clear spike on the UltraEdge when ball is next to bat. Cummins' Test match length does it for SRH and Samson departs for a quick 27. There was a fairly big nick and deflection, so it was a weird review from Samson

dismissalText.commentary: Ruturaj Gaikwad c Malinga b Cummins 15 (21b 0x4 0x6 65m) SR: 71.42

dismissalComment.html: got him!! Gaikwad's struggle ends. Cummins is turning it around. Finally Gaikwad went for a big shot, but couldn't execute. Cummins bowls it short at this counterpart, and has him pulling from around middle stump. Gaikwad pulled with a full flourish, but found the man at deep square leg

dismissalText.commentary: Urvil Patel b Sakib Hussain 13 (8b 0x4 2x6 12m) SR: 162.5

dismissalComment.html: The off stump is knocked back and Urvil is out of here. A third slower ball in a row does the trick. Slower offcutter at 116.7kph that lands back of a length outside off and turns into Urvil. He is through his pull way too early, misses and sees his offpole pegged back

dismissalText.long: c Nitish Kumar Reddy b Cummins
dismissalText.commentary: Kartik Sharma c Nitish Kumar Reddy b Cummins 32 (19b 3x4 2x6 25m) SR: 168.42

dismissalComment.html: Straight down sweeper cover's throat. Cummins has another and SRH have the dangerman in Kartik Sharma. Slower back of a length ball at 117kph outside off. Kartik had the room but not the pace to work with. He cuts up and over, trying to clear the fence on the offside. He can only swipe it as far as Reddy who takes a smart catch running to his right

dismissalText.commentary: Shivam Dube b Sakib Hussain 26 (23b 3x4 1x6 40m) SR: 113.04

dismissalComment.html: the ball keeps low, and he's bowled. Sakib can't stop smiling. Dube looks frustrated. Sakib hits the top of middle stump after going for a short-of-a-length ball on leg. It was a slower one too, and it didn't bounce as much as Dube would've liked. He went for the pull, but was beaten by the lack of pace and bounce

dismissalText.commentary: Prashant Veer c & b Hinge 11 (9b 2x4 0x6 13m) SR: 122.22

dismissalComment.html: taken by Hinge!! He turned around, and went forward to end behind the stumps. It was a good catch, with the ball dropping from over his head. It was a short ball from Hinge, and another slower ball. Veer went pulling, but mistimed it. There was no distance on that, he fell off the last ball

dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson c †Ishan Kishan b Cummins 27 (13b 5x4 1x6 14m) SR: 207.69

dismissalComment.html: Big appeal from Cummins and Kishan for the caught behind and the umpire agrees. Samson isn't sure and reviews. I think I heard a noise. Let's see. On a length just outside off, nibbling away from Samson, who pushes at it and, yep, there is a clear spike on the UltraEdge when ball is next to bat. Cummins' Test match length does it for SRH and Samson departs for a quick 27. There was a fairly big nick and deflection, so it was a weird review from Samson

dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c & b Mukesh Choudhary 6 (6b 1x4 0x6 13m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: Straight up and swallowed by Mukesh! Oh, CSK needed that early and Mukesh Starc Choudhary has landed the first blow. Sharp 142.4kph back of a length ball outside off. dismissalText.commentary: Heinrich Klaasen st †Samson b Noor Ahmad 47 (26b 6x4 2x6 39m) SR: 180.76

dismissalComment.html: Samson is thrilled!! Has he really managed to stump Klaasen here?! We'll wait for the final decision, which will come from the third umpire. Noor angled it across, and went on a fullish length on off. The ball turned away a bit, and Klaasen was beaten on the outside edge as he looked to loft. There's no bat involved there, as confirmed by UltraEdge. Ohh, look at that!! Klaasen's back leg is barely in the air when Samson whips the bails off - almost as instantly as the man watching from the dressing room. This could open the doors for a comeback from CSK

dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c & b Mukesh Choudhary 6 (6b 1x4 0x6 13m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: Straight up and swallowed by Mukesh! Oh, CSK needed that early and Mukesh Starc Choudhary has landed the first blow. Sharp 142.4kph back of a length ball outside off. Head wants to go for a pull, but has to fetch that from way outside off. He only manages a cue-end straight up and Mukesh does the rest. A skip and fist pump from the bowler.

dismissalText.commentary: Abhishek Sharma c Johnson b Hosein 26 (21b 3x4 1x6 40m) SR: 123.8

dismissalComment.html: Straight down deep backward square leg's throat. Left-armer gets left-hander. And out comes Akeal's mask celebration. Darted down leg, was a nothing ball really. Abhishek sweeps and thinks he has got enough power to clear Johnson. But these are big boundaries and Johnson, right on the line takes a comfortable catch. A very un-Abhishek innings comes to an end

dismissalText.commentary: Heinrich Klaasen st †Samson b Noor Ahmad 47 (26b 6x4 2x6 39m) SR: 180.76

dismissalComment.html: Samson is thrilled!! Has he really managed to stump Klaasen here?! We'll wait for the final decision, which will come from the third umpire. Noor angled it across, and went on a fullish length on off. The ball turned away a bit, and Klaasen was beaten on the outside edge as he looked to loft. There's no bat involved there, as confirmed by UltraEdge. Ohh, look at that!! Klaasen's back leg is barely in the air when Samson whips the bails off - almost as instantly as the man watching from the dressing room. This could open the doors for a comeback from CSK

dismissalText.commentary: Nitish Kumar Reddy c Patel b Mukesh Choudhary 11 (7b 1x4 0x6 17m) SR: 157.14

dismissalComment.html: wowww, that seemed to be heading for six!! But Urvil at long-off moved to his right, and grabbed the ball well ahead of the boundary. Choudhary angled the ball across, and bowled on a hittable length wide of off. Reddy went through the line, but got the ball off the bottom of the bat. A wicket and a dot for CSK
